source.title: Screening for Lung Cancer: US Preventive Services Task Force Recommendation Statement

signals: US Preventive Services Task Force Recommendation Statement

extraction.summary: The USPSTF recommends annual lung cancer screening with low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) for adults aged 50-80 years with a 20 pack-year smoking history who currently smoke or quit within the past 15 years. This updates the 2013 recommendation by lowering the starting age from 55 to 50 years and reducing the pack-year requirement from 30 to 20 pack-years.

population.risk_criteria: 20 pack-year smoking history and currently smoke or have quit within the past 15 years
population.disease_definition: Lung cancer - second most common cancer and leading cause of cancer death in the US with overall 5-year survival rate of 20.5%
population.exclusions: Persons who have not smoked for 15 years or have health problems that substantially limit life expectancy or ability/willingness to have curative lung surgery
population.severity_staging: Early-stage lung cancer has better prognosis and is more amenable to treatment

key_thresholds.context: Maximum time since quitting smoking for continued eligibility

key_thresholds.parameter: Years since quitting to discontinue screening

key_thresholds.context: Maximum time since smoking cessation for eligibility

recommendations.recommendation: Annual screening for lung cancer with LDCT in adults aged 50-80 years who have a 20 pack-year smoking history and currently smoke or have quit within the past 15 years
recommendations.notes: Moderate net benefit based on adequate evidence

recommendations.recommendation: Annual screening with low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) for adults aged 50-80 years with ≥20 pack-year smoking history who currently smoke or quit within past 15 years
recommendations.evidence_level: RCT evidence from NLST and NELSON trials
recommendations.notes: NLST showed 20% relative risk reduction in lung cancer mortality; NELSON showed 25% reduction

recommendations.recommendation: Annual screening with low-dose computed tomography for eligible adults
recommendations.notes: Based on National Lung Screening Trial and other randomized controlled trials showing mortality benefit

red_flags: Health problem that substantially limits life expectancy
Health problem that substantially limits ability or willingness to have curative lung surgery
Symptoms suggesting lung cancer in screening-eligible individuals
Positive screen results requiring immediate diagnostic evaluation
Complications from invasive procedures (occurred in 0.1% of screened patients in NLST)
Suspicious lung nodules on LDCT requiring immediate follow-up
extraction.follow_up: Annual screening with LDCT until person has not smoked for 15 years or develops limiting health problems
extraction.escalation_path: Positive screens should be managed using standardized approaches like Lung-RADS classification system, ideally at centers with experience and expertise in lung cancer screening and management of lung lesions detected on LDCT
